title: Soft Body Horse Grooming Simulator
description: The user sees Kira standing beside a horse whose body is covered in a soft-body mesh that deforms realistically when touched. The user must use a brush tool controlled by mouse clicks to groom different zones of the horse while Kira reacts with animations, completing a multi-step grooming routine that tracks pressure, coverage, and technique.
prompt:
Create a single HTML file using Three.js r170 that implements a soft-body horse grooming simulator. Load both GLB assets: place the horse model at center stage and Kira standing to the left facing the horse. Use GLTFLoader from a CDN. Set up a PerspectiveCamera at position (0, 2, 5) looking at the horse, with OrbitControls limited to a 180-degree arc so the user can rotate around the front half. Add a warm directional light simulating afternoon sun, a soft hemisphere light for ambient fill, and a subtle point light near Kira. Implement a soft-body simulation on the horse mesh: subdivide or access the horse geometry vertices and apply spring-damper constraints between neighboring vertices. When the user clicks and drags on the horse surface, use raycasting to find the hit point and apply a gentle displacement force to nearby vertices within a radius, creating a visible soft deformation that springs back over 0.5 seconds. Divide the horse into 6 grooming zones: head, neck, left flank, right flank, back, and hindquarters. Display a UI panel showing zone completion percentages. The interaction steps are: (1) select the brush tool from a floating toolbar, (2) adjust brush pressure using a slider with values 0.1 to 1.0, (3) groom the neck zone to 80% coverage, (4) groom the back zone to 80% coverage, (5) groom both flank zones to 60% each, (6) groom the head zone gently with pressure below 0.4, (7) groom the hindquarters zone, (8) validate all zones reach threshold. Validate that head zone was groomed with average pressure under 0.4 or show a warning. Track cumulative brush strokes per zone. When a zone reaches its threshold, highlight it green on the mesh. When Kira detects grooming activity via proximity of brush to horse, play a subtle idle animation by oscillating her Y rotation slightly. When all zones are complete, trigger a celebration state where both models gently bounce using a sine wave on Y position. Show a completion modal with stats: total strokes, average pressure, and time elapsed. Use requestAnimationFrame for the render loop and update soft-body vertex positions each frame using Verlet integration.

[State Exposure (required)]
Expose window.__3D_STATE__ updated every animation frame:
window.__3D_STATE__ = {
  brushPressure: number,         // current brush pressure setting (0.0 to 1.0)
  activeTool: string,         // currently selected tool name ('brush' or 'none')
  groomedZoneCount: number,         // count of zones that have reached 100% completion
  totalGroomingStrokes: number,         // cumulative number of brush strokes applied across all zones
  headZoneProgress: number,         // grooming completion percentage for head zone (0-100)
  neckZoneProgress: number,         // grooming completion percentage for neck zone (0-100)
  leftFlankZoneProgress: number,         // grooming completion percentage for left flank zone (0-100)
  rightFlankZoneProgress: number,         // grooming completion percentage for right flank zone (0-100)
  backZoneProgress: number,         // grooming completion percentage for back zone (0-100)
  hindquartersZoneProgress: number,         // grooming completion percentage for hindquarters zone (0-100)
  kiraAnimationActive: boolean,         // whether Kira is currently playing a reaction animation
  softBodyDeformationActive: boolean,         // whether the horse mesh is currently deforming from user interaction
}
